data.title: Secret Santa Cycle
data.solution:
import sys

# Read number of players
n = int(input())

# Read player names
players = [input().strip() for _ in range(n)]

# Assign each player to the next one in the list
# The last player points to the first one (creating a cycle)
for i in range(n):
    giver = players[i]
    target = players[(i + 1) % n]
    print(f"{giver} {target}")
data.statement:
You are organizing a <<Secret Santa>> event for [[N]] players.
Each player must give a gift to exactly one other player, forming a single chain that visits everyone exactly once and returns to the start.
<<Assign a target to each player following this rule:>> each player gives to the next one in the input order, and the last player gives to the first.

data.constraints:
3 ≤ [[N]] ≤ 10
1 ≤ length of [[player name]] ≤ 20
All names are distinct and contain only letters [a-zA-Z]
data.stubGenerator:
read N:int
loop N read PLAYER:string(20)
write GIVER TARGET
data.inputDescription:
<<Line 1:>> An integer [[N]] for the number of players.
<<Next [[N]] lines:>> One player name per line, in the order they joined. Names contain only letters and are case-sensitive.
data.outputDescription:
<<[[N]] lines:>> Each line contains two space-separated names: [[giver]] and [[target]].
Each player must appear exactly once as [[giver]].
The output must follow the order of the players as given in the input; any other order will not be valid.

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

You are organizing a Secret Santa event for N players.
Each player must give a gift to exactly one other player, forming a single chain that visits everyone exactly once and returns to the start.
Assign a target to each player following this rule: each player gives to the next one in the input order, and the last player gives to the first.
Input
Line 1: An integer N for the number of players.
Next N lines: One player name per line, in the order they joined. Names contain only letters and are case-sensitive.
Output
N lines: Each line contains two space-separated names: giver and target.
Each player must appear exactly once as giver.
The output must follow the order of the players as given in the input; any other order will not be valid.
Constraints
3 ≤ N ≤ 10
1 ≤ length of player name ≤ 20
All names are distinct and contain only letters [a-zA-Z]
Example
Input
3
Alice
Bob
Charlie
Output
Alice Bob
Bob Charlie
Charlie Alice
